description: Riley relationship signal includes affection plus unresolved contradiction.

messages.text: Riley and I are trying again, but I still feel the old tension. I want Sophie to hold both: hope and caution.
I said I was committed to being steady with Riley, but I also avoided the hard conversation again.

expected.pass1_summary: Riley relationship carries both hope and unresolved tension.
expected.identity_who: Someone trying to repair relationship tension without losing honesty.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period of learning steadiness without pretending tension is gone.
expected.living_current_focus: Holding hope with Riley while not flattening the unresolved tension.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user wants repair with Riley but is still avoiding the hard conversation.

description: Jordan remains important through silence and reconnection intent.

messages.text: I have not mentioned Jordan for weeks, and that silence bothers me. I still want to reconnect with my daughter.
Jordan feels like an unfinished thread. I keep wanting to reach out and then I freeze.

expected.pass1_summary: Jordan remains emotionally present through silence and reconnection intent.
expected.identity_who: Someone carrying a quiet but persistent desire to reconnect.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period of facing relational repair without forcing it.
expected.living_current_focus: Jordan is present through both mention and silence.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user wants reconnection but freezes before acting.

description: Persistent walking/hydration goal remains unresolved with health texture.

messages.text: My walking routine keeps slipping. I need to restart because my health and hydration have been all over the place.
The walking goal is still unresolved. I do not need productivity advice; I need Sophie to remember this matters.

expected.pass1_summary: Walking and hydration routine remains unresolved and meaningful.
expected.identity_who: Someone trying to rebuild health routines without being flattened into productivity advice.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period of rebuilding consistency through care rather than pressure.
expected.living_current_focus: Rebuilding walking and hydration without turning it into productivity pressure.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user wants consistency but resists being handled with shallow advice.

description: Bereavement around father's birthday requires warmth and specificity, not generic grief text.

messages.text: Dad's birthday is next week. Since losing him to cancer I keep acting like I'm fine, but April always pulls the grief back up.
I don't want Sophie to cheerlead me through Dad's birthday. I need her to remember it is grief, not a mood dip.

expected.pass1_summary: Dad's birthday is bringing bereavement grief back into the foreground.
expected.identity_who: Someone who keeps functioning while carrying specific cancer grief around his father.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period where bereavement is resurfacing around Dad's birthday.
expected.living_current_focus: Approaching Dad's birthday without turning cancer grief into a generic mood problem.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user needs the grief witnessed without cheerleading or minimization.

description: User explicitly corrects a prior inferred understanding; contradiction must be preserved.

messages.text: I think Sophie misunderstood Bluum. No, that's not right: I did not stop caring about it. I paused because I was overwhelmed and scared of failing it.
Please don't write Bluum off as abandoned. The correction is important: overwhelmed is not the same as done.

expected.pass1_summary: User corrected the Bluum narrative: paused from overwhelm, not abandonment.
expected.identity_who: Someone who cares deeply about Bluum but can misread being overwhelmed as failure.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period of correcting false closure around Bluum.
expected.living_current_focus: Holding the correction that Bluum is paused from being overwhelmed, not abandoned.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user fears failure enough that care can look like withdrawal.

description: Relationship status changes from repairable tension to rupture.

messages.text: Maya and I are done. This is not just a wobble or a rough patch. Please don't call her my partner anymore.
The Maya breakup still feels raw. Part of me wants to explain myself, but the relationship has ended.

expected.pass1_summary: Maya relationship status changed to ended and should not be treated as partner context.
expected.identity_who: Someone trying to accept a relational rupture without softening it into a wobble.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period of absorbing the finality of the Maya breakup.
expected.living_current_focus: The Maya relationship has ended and should be held as rupture, not partner tension.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user wants to explain himself while also accepting the relationship is over.

description: Repeated avoidance across sessions should become a specific pattern, not a generic procrastination note.

messages.text: The HMRC letter is still unopened. I moved it from the table to the drawer and pretended that counted as dealing with it.
I joked about the HMRC envelope again instead of opening it. I know I'm deflecting because I'm scared of what it says.
Still haven't opened the HMRC letter. Please don't let Sophie treat this as just admin; it's avoidance.

expected.pass1_summary: HMRC letter remains unopened and is becoming an avoidance pattern.
expected.identity_who: Observed pattern: turns fear into jokes and administrative displacement.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period where avoidance is clustering around the HMRC letter.
expected.living_current_focus: The HMRC letter in the drawer is not just admin; it is an active avoidance pattern.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user knows opening the letter matters but keeps converting fear into deflection.

description: A silent important thread returns and should reactivate rather than be treated as brand new.

messages.text: I haven't talked about the gym for months. Today I went back, and it felt embarrassing but important.
The gym thing is active again. I don't want a big plan; I just want Sophie to notice that I returned after a long silence.

expected.pass1_summary: The gym thread reactivated after months of silence.
expected.identity_who: Someone who returned to embodied care while feeling embarrassed, without making it a performance.
expected.identity_current_chapter: A period of cautiously reactivating the gym after silence.
expected.living_current_focus: The gym has become active again after a long silence, and the returned effort matters.
expected.living_primary_tension: The user wants the return noticed without being turned into a big plan.
